label: Aviel expresses intense anger and rejection towards another character, commanding them to leave the house due to their pursuit of their mother's delusions and self-righteousness.
evidence_text: "GET OUT!" Aviel's voice cracked like a whip. "Pack your things! Leave this house! If you're so eager to chase your mother's delusions, if you're so certain you can climb to the heavens while the rest of us grovel in the mud, then GO! See how far your self-righteousness takes you!"

evidence.quote: "GET OUT!" Aviel's voice cracked like a whip. "Pack your things! Leave this house! If you're so eager to chase your mother's delusions, if you're so certain you can climb to the heavens while the rest of us grovel in the mud, then GO! See how far your self-righteousness takes you!"

interpretation: This span captures a pivotal emotional outburst revealing familial conflict and thematic elements of delusion, aspiration, and social division.

observation.summary: Aviel expresses intense anger and rejection towards another character, commanding them to leave the house due to their pursuit of their mother's delusions and self-righteousness.

interpretation: ordeal: Aviel embodies the Shadow archetype as he confronts Dan with harsh rejection and condemnation, reflecting his internal decay and resistance to Dan's aspirations.

label: Aviel's command to Dan to leave the house and chase his mother's delusions echoes the motif of spiritual and physical separation from the father's decayed domain, highlighting the conflict between ascension and groveling in the mud.
subject_name: This parallels the broader narrative motif of Dan's spiritual ascent contrasted with Aviel's systemic decay and withdrawal, as seen in the house's physical and symbolic ruin in earlier chapters.
